verdicts.reason: The chart rendering logic is robust, handles various edge cases with appropriate warnings, and sanitizes user input to prevent injection.
The chart feature correctly sanitizes and escapes user input for SVG rendering, adhering to existing architectural constraints regarding innerHTML and local-only content.
The chart block feature is exceptionally well-designed with clear examples, robust error feedback, and accessibility considerations, ensuring a frictionless user experience.
The chart feature is well-documented in the AI prompt guide, includes a live example in the default markdown, and provides informative in-app warnings for usage issues.
Inline chart blocks solve a real problem for data visualization in markdown-first presentations, integrating data directly into the source and respecting themes.
verdicts.evidence: Users frequently need to display data in presentations; this feature eliminates the need for external tools and manual image embedding, making the workflow significantly more efficient for data-driven slides. The handling of edge cases (negative values, data limits, invalid input) suggests it's built for realistic use, not just demos.

verdicts.reason: The inline, theme-aware SVG chart generation from simple CSV data is a strong signature move, enhancing the tool's unique zero-dependency value proposition.

[AUTO-DOWNGRADED: LESSONS VETO missing precondition_evidence] The 'Named Snapshots' feature, and other modals persisting user data to localStorage, violate the documented lesson requiring a user-facing storage disclaimer.
verdicts.required_fix: Add a 'Stored locally on this device. Not synced. Not encrypted.' disclaimer within the 'Named Snapshots' modal, and similar modals ('Kiosk', 'Budget') that persist user data to localStorage.
verdicts.evidence: KEEP: localStorage disclaimer required whenever a drawer persists data — Any drawer that saves user data to localStorage must include a 'Stored locally on this device. Not synced. Not encrypted.' disclaimer, matching the pattern established by #drafts-disclaimer and #addr-disclaimer. GUIDE will object if a new drawer adds persistence (e.g., after escalation resolution) without disclosing it. Add the disclaimer at the same time as the persistence code.
